explainability.findings: Policy documents state a commitment to explaining adverse decisions and the AI systems used. These documents also state individuals have the right to request information about AI data influencing decisions.
explainability.path_to_improvement: Publish user-facing explanation interfaces or documented appeal workflows.

relevant_sources.summary: This privacy policy document provides evidence for **explainability, governance, and transparency**. It commits to explaining adverse decisions and the AI systems used, and grants individuals the right to request information about AI data influencing decisions, directly supporting explainability and transparency. Furthermore, the mention of AI systems and their use in underwriting and pricing indicates transparency and governance over the company's AI usage.
relevant_sources.title: Privacy Policy

external_accountability.path_to_improvement: Obtain external validation of AI practices or require vendor certifications.

fairness.path_to_improvement: Document bias testing procedures or vendor AI fairness requirements.

governance.findings: An article describes an enterprise AI governance program that outlines a formal AI governance structure, references a Generative AI Policy, and specifies required training for all associates. This program also outlines the designation of 'AI Champions' within business units to lead responsible implementation and identify opportunities. Additionally, policy documents reference AI systems and their use in underwriting and pricing.
governance.path_to_improvement: Name an AI governance body with defined mandate covering all AI use.

relevant_sources.summary: This article details Western & Southern Financial Group's enterprise AI governance program, providing evidence for **governance** and **transparency**. The program establishes a formal AI governance structure with a Generative AI Policy and required training for all associates, demonstrating a commitment to responsible AI use. Furthermore, the designation of 'AI Champions' within business units to lead responsible implementation and identify opportunities highlights a structured approach to AI management and accountability.
relevant_sources.title: Training MVP Awards Best Practice: Western & Southern Financial Group's Generative Artificial Intelligence Training Program

relevant_sources.summary: This privacy policy document provides evidence for **explainability, governance, and transparency**. It commits to explaining adverse decisions and the AI systems used, and grants individuals the right to request information about AI data influencing decisions, directly supporting explainability and transparency. Furthermore, the mention of AI systems and their use in underwriting and pricing indicates transparency and governance over the company's AI usage.
relevant_sources.title: Privacy Policy

oversight.path_to_improvement: Document human review processes for AI-assisted decisions (built or vendor AI).

privacy.path_to_improvement: Document data protection practices for AI systems, including vendor AI data handling.

transparency.findings: Policy documents state a commitment to explaining the AI systems used and outline individuals' right to request information about AI data influencing decisions. These documents also reference AI systems and their use in underwriting and pricing.
transparency.path_to_improvement: Publish detailed documentation (model cards, system specs) for AI systems deployed.

relevant_sources.summary: This article details Western & Southern Financial Group's enterprise AI governance program, providing evidence for **governance** and **transparency**. The program establishes a formal AI governance structure with a Generative AI Policy and required training for all associates, demonstrating a commitment to responsible AI use. Furthermore, the designation of 'AI Champions' within business units to lead responsible implementation and identify opportunities highlights a structured approach to AI management and accountability.
relevant_sources.title: Training MVP Awards Best Practice: Western & Southern Financial Group's Generative Artificial Intelligence Training Program

relevant_sources.summary: This privacy policy document provides evidence for **explainability, governance, and transparency**. It commits to explaining adverse decisions and the AI systems used, and grants individuals the right to request information about AI data influencing decisions, directly supporting explainability and transparency. Furthermore, the mention of AI systems and their use in underwriting and pricing indicates transparency and governance over the company's AI usage.

summary.overall_findings: Western & Southern Financial Group's policy documents state a commitment to explaining the AI systems used and outline individuals' right to request information about AI data influencing decisions. Additionally, published materials describe an enterprise AI governance program that outlines a formal AI governance structure, contributing to the 3 of 7 evaluated responsible AI pillars with documented evidence. Explainability is also addressed at the policy level, with commitments to explaining adverse decisions, based on a review of 10 publicly available sources. No qualifying public evidence was found for fairness, oversight, and 2 additional pillars.
